'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takamefu-jinja}} [[JA:高売布神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669105}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takamefu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:三田・高売布神社.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}} Sakai 50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|31.0|N|135|16|1.0|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamefu Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takamefu-jinja}} [[JA:高売布神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669105}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takamefu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:三田・高売布神社.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}} Sakai 50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|31.0|N|135|16|1.0|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamefu Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]

There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takamefu-jinja}} [[JA:高売布神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669105}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takamefu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:三田・高売布神社.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}} Sakai 50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|31.0|N|135|16|1.0|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamefu Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takamefu-jinja}} [[JA:高売布神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669105}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takamefu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:三田・高売布神社.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}} Sakai 50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|31.0|N|135|16|1.0|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamefu Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Around {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Takamefu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[画像:三田・高売布神社本殿.JPG|thumb|160px|Honden (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Takamefu Shrine''' (たかめふじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sanda|Q847607|WD=Q847607|1=Sanda, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the only {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Sanda City, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of former {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}. The honden and wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are designated national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahime-no-Kami), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} as associated deity. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. There is also a theory relating it to the founding of {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}}, another shikinaisha in the same Kawabe District. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, who held vast estates in the region, is said to have made it the district sōsha. There is a tradition that Mitsunaka planted a {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree. In {{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|WD=Q6831|1=1626}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3), it became a hereditary prayer place for the Aoki clan, lords of Asada Domain. In {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1), through research by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} (ordered to compile local geography by temple-and-shrine magistrate {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}}), this shrine was identified as the shikinaisha Takamefu Shrine, and thereafter came to be called Takamefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked gōsha; at the end of Meiji it integrated and merged shrines from Sakai, Tokura, Tanaka, Kawahara, Nunogi, and Sueyoshi villages, forming the present shrine. At some period it was also called Kazuki Daimyōjin, Tsuki-no-miya, and Sai-no-miya. A shrine-name plaque reading “Senior First Rank Kazuki Daimyōjin,” donated from {{ill|lt=Hōkyō-ji|Q11452697|WD=Q11452697|1=Hōkyō-ji Temple}} in Kyoto, remains in the haiden. === Shrine buildings and precincts === *Honden: Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=hinoki-bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}; original construction date unknown. A ridge tag indicates rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200|1=1513}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 10). *Wooden komainu: carved from single blocks of {{ill|lt=kaya wood|Q1373999|WD=Q1373999|1=Torreya nucifera}}, with inscription dated {{ill|lt=1297|Q5562|WD=Q5562|1=1297}} (Einin 5). *Subsidiary shrines: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} Shrine and {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} Shrine. === Rituals and annual events === *Senbon-tsuki: on October 7, fathers called "hatsutō" (whose children were born in the past year) sing "Sosoba-tsuki-uta" while making "chigiri-mikyo," rice cakes offered at the annual festival. *Annual festival: on October 9, "chigiri-mikyo" is offered before the deity, and "hatsutō" carry the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national)=== *Honden (with ridge tag and former kashiranuki beam) *One pair of wooden komainu === Location / access === 50 Miyanowaki, Sakai, Sanda, Hyōgo Prefecture === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kanfuku-ji (Sanda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfuku-ji}} *{{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)|Q3304192|WD=Q3304192|lt=Mefu Shrine|1=Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:三田市の建築物]] [[Category:三田市の歴史]]
